input.pr_title: #12576 upgrade mypy to 1.19.1, enhance type annotations, and give twisted.internet.reactor a type
input.pr_body:
## Scope and purpose

Fixes #12576 
Fixes #9909 

The scope exploded a little bit there.

diff --git a/src/twisted/internet/__init__.py b/src/twisted/internet/__init__.py

--- a/src/twisted/internet/__init__.py
+++ b/src/twisted/internet/__init__.py
@@ -10,3 +10,30 @@
 to use the same code for different loops, from Twisted's basic, yet portable,
 select-based loop to the loops of various GUI toolkits like GTK+ or Tk.
 """
+
+from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
+
+if TYPE_CHECKING:
+    from .interfaces import (
+        IReactorCore,
+        IReactorFDSet,
+        IReactorProcess,
+        IReactorTCP,
+        IReactorTime,
+    )
+
+    class _IReactorCommon(
+        IReactorCore,
+        IReactorTime,
+        IReactorFDSet,
+        IReactorTCP,
+        IReactorProcess,
+    ):
+        """
+        A make-believe interface supporting all the commonly-implemented parts
+        of the reactor.  For other interfaces which may or may not be supplied
+        by the environment, i.e. IReactorSSL, adapt the reactor like
+        C{IReactorSSL(reactor)}.
+        """
+
+    reactor: _IReactorCommon

diff --git a/src/twisted/newsfragments/12120.bugfix b/src/twisted/newsfragments/12120.bugfix
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..815c1c12015
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/twisted/newsfragments/12120.bugfix
@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
+A potential reference cycle that might cause intermittent memory spikes while
+using twisted.internet.defer.inlineCallbacks was removed.

input.pr_title: #12580 web: update type annotations for Python 3.9+ (1/1)
input.pr_body:
## Scope and purpose

Fixes #12580

The changes were automatically generated using:

* `pyupgrade --py39-plus .\src\twisted\web\*.py`
* `pyupgrade --py39-plus .\src\twisted\web\*\*.py`

Manual work done:

* Add `from __future__ import annotations` where appropriate
* Remove unused imports from typing module such as List. (`pyupgrade` doesn't remove these)

## Contributor Checklist:

This process applies to *all* pull requests - no matter how small.
Have a look at [our developer documentation](https://docs.twisted.org/en/latest/core/development/dev-process.html) before submitting your Pull Request.

Below is a non-exhaustive list (as a reminder):

* The title of the PR should describe the changes and starts with the associated issue number, like “#1234 Brief description”.
* A release notes news fragment file was create in src/twisted/newsfragments/ (see: [Release notes fragments docs.](https://docs.twisted.org/en/latest/core/development/dev-process.html#release-notes-management))
* The automated tests were updated.
* Once all checks are green, request a review by leaving a comment that contains exactly the string `please review`.
  Our bot will trigger the review process, by applying the pending review label
  and requesting a review from the Twisted dev team.

+            exposedThrough: list[Expose] = getattr(fObj, "exposedThrough", [])

         if loader is not None:
             self.loader = loader
 
     def lookupRenderMethod(
         self, name: str
-    ) -> Callable[[Optional[IRequest], "Tag"], "Flattenable"]:

         Look up and return the named render method.

         """
         Implement L{IRenderable} to allow one L{Element} to be embedded in
         another's template or rendering output.

     """
     Within comments the sequence C{-->} can be mistaken as the end of the comment.

 ) -> Flattenable:
     """
     Find the value of the named slot in the given stack of slot data.
@@ -224,16 +212,16 @@ def errback(failure: Failure) -> Failure:

-    request: Optional[IRequest],
+    request: IRequest | None,
     root: Flattenable,
     write: Callable[[bytes], object],

-    renderFactory: Optional[IRenderable],

     # This is annotated as Generator[T, None, None] instead of Iterator[T]
